---
title: "kasdhflaksjdhflaskjfh"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggbeeswarm)

read_data <- read.csv("../data/test_data/data.csv")

team_list<- c("449","2988")

# calculates avg n times a team crossed bump: 
#avg_tbump <- read_data |> 
#group_by(team) |>
#summarize(mean_val = round(mean(teleop_bump), 2))  |> 
#arrange(desc(mean_val))
# print(avg_tbump)

filtered_df <- read_data |> filter(team %in% team_list)

df_bump <- filtered_df |>
    select(team, count = teleop_bump) |> 
    group_by(team) |>
    mutate(obstacle = "Bump",
           avg = mean(count))

df_trench <- filtered_df |> 
    select(team, count = teleop_trench) |> 
    group_by(team) |>
    mutate(obstacle = "Trench",
           avg = mean(count))

combined_df <- rbind(df_trench, df_bump) |>
    group_by(team, obstacle) |>
    summarize(avg_trench = ifelse(obstacle == "Trench", avg, 0),
              avg_bump = ifelse(obstacle == "Bump", avg, 0)) |>
    unique() 
    
combined_more <- combined_df |>
    group_by(team) |>
    summarize(
        avg_trench1 = sum(avg_trench),
        avg_bump1 = sum(avg_bump)
    )

ggplot(combined_more, aes(x = avg_trench1, y = avg_bump1)) + 
    geom_point() +
    geom_label(
    label = combined_more$team,
    nudge_x = 0.25, nudge_y = 0.25,
    check_overlap = T
  ) +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0, max(combined_more$avg_trench1 + 1))) + 
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0, max(combined_more$avg_bump1 + 1))) +
    labs(title = "Mean Crossing Comparison",
         x = "Trench count",
         y = "Bump count") + 
    theme_minimal()
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
